response.justification: Wei Chen is a broadly experienced full-stack and systems developer with strong TDD, microservices, and polyglot engineering credentials, but is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The minimum requirement of 8 years of C++ experience is the single largest disqualifier: the resume shows roughly 6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis reference, with C++ absent entirely from the skills section. The role also demands 5 years of large-scale infrastructure or cloud compute platform work, and while the candidate has touched AWS CDK and Kubernetes peripherally, there is no evidence of owning or leading infrastructure at that depth or scale. The technical leadership experience present is informal (dojos, talks, open-source founding) rather than the structured mentoring of cloud or DevOps engineering teams the role requires.
strengths: Demonstrated polyglot engineering depth with production experience across Node.js, TypeScript, D language, and microservices handling millions of requests per second at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby
Strong software craftsmanship culture: consistent TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and community teaching through workshops and open-source contributions
Staff-level tenure and founding experience (GISCollective, Altom) show capacity for ambiguous, long-horizon technical ownership
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015), directly failing the 8-year minimum qualification with no path to bridge the gap

No meaningful cloud compute platform or distributed infrastructure ownership; AWS CDK at Optilyz and Kubernetes for a small open-source project do not constitute 5 years of large-scale infrastructure work
No formal technical leadership record mentoring cloud or DevOps engineers; leadership evidence is community-facing (workshops, talks) rather than organizational team leadership

key_factors.factor: Engineering craftsmanship and polyglot systems background
